programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: The 90s dance floor was a place to dream, fantasise and lose yourself to the music. Vernon mixes up the best dream-themed tunes from La Bouche, Mariah Carey, D:Ream and Baby D.
The Disco Queen is ready and waiting with two hours of the best in party tunes. This week there's music from The Wiseguys, Pink, Texas, Pitbull and... Eddie Murphy!

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Whether you need to unravel or focus your mind, Angela Griffin has the playlist for you. Featuring relaxing music from the likes of Rick Astley, Elkie Brooks and Toni Braxton.
Legendary singer-songwriter Carole King performing live at the Royal Albert Hall in 1989. Featuring Home Again, So Far Away, I Feel The Earth Move and You've Got A Friend.
Singer, actor and Love Songs presenter Michael Ball joins Mark Goodier for an in-depth conversation about his life, choosing ten songs which have soundtracked that time so far.
The Reverend Kate Bottley celebrates Easter Sunday with some live music from Jerub, and Harry Clark reflects on his recent journey to Rome, in a quest to meet the new Pope.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Michael... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy's live from Salford and he wants to know about your alternate easter traditions! Plus, The Weekest Link quiz, Paddy's Timewarp and your Sunday Dinners.
Elaine's Easter Special, with special show tunes for Easter, your Break a Leg messages, and some of the musical theatre performers and creatives nominated for 2026 Olivier Awards.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 70s with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Shaun Keaveny sits in. marking the anniversary of Johnnie Walker's first Sounds of the 70s, this day in 2009, plus more archive action from the Rock Date Diary.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1989.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1995.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
The Ballad of Gambo and Hollywood Icons in Song. Plus, music from Abba, Julie Andrews, Gene Pitney & The Monkees.
Angela invites you to unwind and relax with a calming soundtrack for this bank holiday weekend, featuring music from Chaka Khan, Justin Bieber and Seal.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela invites you to unwind and relax with a playlist of sweet treats for Easter, featuring Elton John, Emeli Sandé and Cleo Sol.
Richie Anderson is live from Salford with your Monday Motivation to kick-start a brand new week, plus Secret Star and This Week in Songs.

programmes.show_name: Alternative Sounds of the 90s with Dermot O'Leary
programmes.show_description: Dermot picks his favourite 90s albums which include The Color and The Shape, Urban Hymns and Automatic For The People. Plus, Kylie picks her favourite 90s band for us.
It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems -  Owain is asking about your first pets and advice on being a first time dog owner!
OJ has your morning sorted with Sinitta and music to get you up and out!
Making your mid-mornings memorable, Gary's bringing big tunes, big names and big laughs!
Tony lights up bank holiday Monday with a live Soulful celebration of the very best music, including Luther Vandross, Candi Staton, Beverley Knight, George Benson & Ce Ce Peniston.
Mark Goodier presents his essential selection of the finest 'billion streamers' from the past six decades of rock and pop.
Vernon Kay presents highlights of the performances from this year's Piano Room Month, accompanied by the BBC Concert Orchestra in the BBC's Maida Vale studios.
Jo Whiley introduces Pulp performing in front of a small audience at the BBC Radio Theatre.

programmes.show_name: The Blues Show with Cerys Matthews
programmes.show_description: Grammy-nominated Malian singer-songwriter, guitarist, and actress, Fatoumata Diawara pops in to chat about her new music and to share her favourite blues records and influences.

programmes.show_name: The Good Groove with DJ Spoony
programmes.show_description: Feel-good dancefloor favourites and soul gems, all five minutes in duration and longer to make you groove! Super luxurious tuneage to round up your bank holiday weekend!
